title: Cyrtopera massokoensis
taxonomy: Cyrtopera massokoensis is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Cynochloraeae. This classification places it among other orchid species that share similar characteristics and growth habits. Within the Orchidaceae family, Cyrtopera massokoensis is further divided into various ranks, including genus, species, and infraspecific levels. The classification of Cyrtopera massokoensis is based on its morphological features, including its flower structure and leaf arrangement.

intro: Cyrtopera massokoensis, also known as Massoko orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential effects on wound healing and the treatment of various diseases. Bioactive constituents of Cyrtopera massokoensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a powder or decoction and used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
